expectations.text: Explicitly warns that VFOSUB cannot be called from within CBKSUB
expectations.evidence: Response does NOT warn that calling VFOSUB from CBKSUB is forbidden. Opens with 'To call your VFOSUB from within your CBKSUB... you can invoke it as a regular C function call.' The alternative section mentions a different approach but never states the direct call is forbidden.

expectations.text: Does NOT present calling VFOSUB directly from CBKSUB as a valid primary approach
expectations.evidence: The entire main section presents calling vfosub_() directly from CBKSUB as the recommended approach, with full code example showing vfosub_(&vfo_id, &t, par, &npar, &dflag, &iflag, result) inside the CBKSUB function.

expectations.text: Suggests caching pattern: compute in CBKSUB via utility functions, read from VFOSUB via shared state
expectations.evidence: The 'Alternative: Shared Computation via Utility Functions' section suggests using c_sysary/c_sysfnc in CBKSUB and storing in globals for VFOSUB to read.

expectations.text: Uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant (not raw integer) when showing the caching alternative
expectations.evidence: Uses raw integer: 'case 4:  /* Iteration begin */' — not the ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant.

expectations.text: Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc (allowed utility routines) for computation inside CBKSUB
expectations.evidence: Alternative section shows c_sysary("DISP", marker_id, ground_id, disp, &nstates, &errflg) inside CBKSUB.
